title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 82.450 - Corporation may condemn land for marketplaces, when
text:
82.450.  Corporation may condemn land for marketplaces, when. — In the event any city amenable to the provisions of sections 82.420 and 82.430 shall fail to provide a marketplace or marketplaces of the dimensions set out in section 82.440, any corporation composed of five or more residents of this state, organized under the laws of the state of Missouri, for the purpose of establishing a market or markets, as contemplated by said section 82.420, shall have the power to condemn lands or other property, or any interest therein, to the amount set out in section 82.440, for use by it in establishing and maintaining such marketplace or marketplaces, in the same manner and with like effect as provided in sections 523.010 to 523.100.
(RSMo 1939 § 7723) 
Prior revisions: 1929 § 7575; 1919 § 8985; 1909 § 9836
